'''Jula Shryne''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] from [[Weytta]] and the [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] captain of the ''[[Drunk Dancer]]''.

==Biography==
Jula was [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]], but her parents deliberately hid her from the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]]. Her son, [[Roan Shryne]], was likewise Force-sensitive, and Jula's husband [[Jen Shryne]] gave Roan to the Jedi against her will during one of her business trips. As a result, she left Jen.

In [[25 BBY/Legends|25 BBY]], Jula was one of many freelancers contacted by [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]], under his Sith alias Tyranus, to run communications gear to a complex on [[Jaguada's moon]]. Not knowing Tyranus's true identity, or the true intent of the base, she took up the job.

Following [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]], Jula was briefly reunited with Roan, taking him and other Jedi survivors aboard the ''Drunk Dancer''. During a mission on [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]], she was injured by [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader's]] [[Telekinesis/Legends#Saber throw|thrown]] [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] as she and her crew were helping [[Fang Zar/Legends|Fang Zar]] to escape to his [[Sern Prime/Legends|home planet]] aboard their vessel. She survived her injuries and tried convincing Roan to join her crew. Roan, in the end, stayed true to his Jedi nature, giving his life on [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]] to distract Vader long enough for Jula and others to escape the system.

==Appearances==
*''[[Dark Lord: The Rise of Darth Vader]]'' {{Ab|Dark Lord: The Rise of Darth Vader (unabridged audiobook)}} {{1st|in book}}
